heroBody: An interactive investigation into Africa's accelerating land degradation crisis. Scroll to explore the story.
stepOverviewBody: More than 65% of Africa's productive agricultural land has been degraded. This crisis threatens food security for over 1.3 billion people across the continent.
stepSahelBody: The Sahel — stretching 5,400 km from Senegal to Eritrea — loses millions of hectares of topsoil each year to wind erosion and desertification.
stepEthiopiaBody: Rapid deforestation in the Ethiopian highlands has accelerated erosion, with some watersheds losing up to 200 tonnes of soil per hectare annually.
stepSouthernBody: Climate change has extended drought cycles across southern Africa, compounding existing land degradation and displacing entire communities.
comparisonDescription: Drag the handle to compare vegetation cover in the Sahel region between 2010 and 2024.
pullQuoteDesertification: hectares lost each year to desertification
ctaDescription: Use our interactive map to explore detailed statistics for every African nation. Draw custom areas, compare layers, and generate reports.
